payload.description: CHAR_B: "So. Tell me what I'm doing wrong."

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: So. Tell me what I'm doing wrong.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You're asking like I might sugarcoat it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Are you going to sugarcoat it?

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You're putting too much work into it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Into what.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Into being noticed.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: ...I do not appreciate how accurate that is.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Okay but what if I just. Explained myself better.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: To who.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: To everyone.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: That is not a plan. That is a vibe. And not a good vibe.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Say the thing you actually think.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I do say it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You say the version that you think people want to hear.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: ...That's not — okay. Okay. That's it, isn't it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Sorry — am I interrupting something. I can leave.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: We're having a moment.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Ooh. What kind.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: The kind where you leave.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: For the record, I heard all of it from the hallway.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Oh no.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: The thing you said about the version people want to hear? That was the thing. That was so the thing.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Why are there suddenly two of you.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Say it. To both of us. The actual thing.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I don't like when people look at me and decide who I am before I open my mouth.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: There it is.

beats.emotion: lowkey devastated (and also kind of proud)

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I shouldn't have said that out loud.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Why not.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Because now it's real.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: ...That's kind of the whole point though, right?

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Don't look at me. I'm not softening this.

state.conditioning_notes: `<CHAR_A>`'s love of calling it straight and fear of being managed or softened drive the entire mentor posture — they refuse to cushion feedback, resist `<CHAR_B>`'s attempts to redirect into easier territory, and signal the lesson through absence of comfort rather than presence of warmth. `<CHAR_B>`'s love of being understood and fear of being overlooked are why they keep reformulating ("what if I explained myself better") — they're not hearing the feedback yet, they're still negotiating for comprehension, until the real admission surfaces. `<CHAR_C>`'s love of the next unknown and fear of being stuck means they drift in, hear something interesting, and stay — not to help, but because a stuck moment is a puzzle and they can't walk away from a puzzle. The home location holds the sequence in an ordinary pocket of time — no stakes imposed by environment, which means all stakes are internal, making the small admissions land heavier than they would anywhere else. The rising arc is driven not by external event but by `<CHAR_B>` getting incrementally less defended, which is the appropriate shape for a mentor_student relationship set in a domestic baseline.
